info.name: ALFRED API
info.description:
ALFRED — JARVIS-like AI butler for Home Assistant.

Base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8099

ALFRED is a streaming OpenAI-compatible proxy that enriches requests with personality, memory, and home layout before forwarding to Claude Sonnet.

request.description: Returns server status, version, and configured LLM model. Use this to verify ALFRED is running.

Lists available models. Returns 'alfred-brain' — the model ID that Custom Conversation uses during setup validation. This is a dummy endpoint; ALFRED always forwards to Claude Sonnet regardless of model name.

Basic conversation test. ALFRED will respond in character as a British butler. The response is SSE (Server-Sent Events) streamed. Each chunk is a 'data:' line with an OpenAI-format JSON object. Stream ends with 'data: [DONE]'.

Tests that the Home Layout Index is being injected. ALFRED should know your floors, rooms, and which devices are where — without you telling it. The layout is fetched from HA's template API on startup.

Tests memory storage. ALFRED stores this conversation in SQLite. After 5 conversations, it extracts facts like 'User's name is Salih' and 'User prefers thermostat at 72°F' using Claude Haiku, embeds them, and stores for future semantic recall.

Tests memory recall. If you previously stored a preference (and enough conversations have occurred for fact extraction), ALFRED should remember it via semantic similarity search. Note: facts are extracted every 5 conversations, so you may need to send several messages first.

Simulates what Custom Conversation actually sends to ALFRED. Includes:
- A system prompt (ALFRED prepends its persona + memory + layout before this)
- HA Assist API tools (HassTurnOn, HassTurnOff, HassGetState)
- stream: true (always)
- stream_options with include_usage (Custom Conversation sends this)

Claude should respond with a tool_call (HassTurnOn for kitchen lights). In the real pipeline, Custom Conversation would execute this and loop back. Here you'll just see the raw tool_call in the SSE stream.

Look for 'finish_reason': 'tool_calls' in the final chunk — that's how Custom Conversation knows to execute tools.
Tests multi-turn conversation. Includes previous messages in the conversation history. In the real pipeline, Custom Conversation manages the conversation history — here we simulate it manually.
ALFRED always streams regardless of this flag. Custom Conversation always sends stream:true, but this tests that ALFRED handles the stream:false case gracefully (it still streams SSE back).
